title: Half Adders and Full Adders
summary: Build arithmetic circuits from gates and understand how carry lets adders scale to multiple bits.
content_html:
A combinational circuit has outputs determined only by the current inputs. Adders are combinational circuits used to add binary digits.

A half adder adds two input bits and produces two outputs: a sum bit and a carry bit. The sum is computed with XOR, and the carry is computed with AND.

Half adder
S  = X xor Y
Co = X . Y

A full adder adds three input bits: X, Y, and carry-in. It produces a sum and a carry-out. Full adders can be built from two half adders plus an OR gate for the carry output.

Full adder
S  = X xor Y xor Ci
Co = (X . Y) + (Ci . (X xor Y))

To add multi-bit numbers, connect adders in a chain. The carry-out from one bit position becomes the carry-in for the next more significant bit. A 4-bit adder uses one adder stage per bit.

exercises.title: Half adder sum
exercises.prompt: Which gate calculates the sum output of a half adder?

exercises.title: Half adder carry
exercises.prompt: Which expression gives the carry output of a half adder with inputs X and Y?

exercises.title: Carry chain
exercises.prompt: In a multi-bit adder, what output from one stage is connected to the next stage's carry-in?

exercises.title: Full adder inputs
exercises.prompt: How many input bits does a full adder add?
